high_consensus.topic: AC-7 / golden replay event sequence is incomplete
high_consensus.consensus: AC-7's resolver event sequence misses CardCommitted (UI-or-command-emitted) AND DaemonReacted (resolver-or-event-emitted). Validation_rules.md golden replay declares the full stream as CardCommitted → ZoneActivated → ZoneEventStarted → DaemonReacted (+ RewardGranted from event resolver steps).
high_consensus.integration: Update AC-7 to assert full 5-event stream. Add to FR-13: define which layer emits CardCommitted (recommend: command-queue on accepted enqueue) and DaemonReacted (recommend: event-resolver-step-derived when daemon present at zone). Update FR-16 sequencer trigger to be event-source-agnostic.

high_consensus.consensus: telemetry.card_activation_clarity.yaml declares ONE event (CardActivationClarity) with 7 aggregate properties, not 4 separate events keyed to semantic beats. PRD AC-13 mistakes the shape.
high_consensus.integration: Rewrite AC-13: emit ONE CardActivationClarity record at sequence-end (unlock_input beat) with the 7 declared properties populated from semantic events. JSONL trail destination only if D11/Q-SDD-5 is resolved now; otherwise cut FR-26 from S5 until SDD.

high_consensus.consensus: Sequence beats target 5 declared anchors PLUS actors (actor.kaori_chibi), daemons (daemon.wood_puruhani_primary), UI surfaces (ui.reward_preview), audio buses (audio.bus.sfx). 100% anchor-binding test is meaningless without distinguishing target classes.
high_consensus.integration: Replace AC-8 wording: anchor-required beats must resolve declared anchors; actor/daemon/UI/audio targets resolve through separate registries. SDD §1 specs the 4-registry shape (Anchor / Actor / UI-mount / Audio-bus).

operator_decisions_pending.question: How to resolve the 5-zone-map requirement vs 1 vendored zone YAML
options: A: Add 4 placeholder zone YAMLs (water_harbor + fire_station + metal_mountain + earth_teahouse) authored from element naming conventions, marked affectsGameplay: false equivalent
B: Rewrite AC-10/FR-20 to '1 schema-backed wood_grove + 4 decorative locked tiles' rendering as zone placeholders without YAML backing
C: Reduce vertical slice to 1 zone visible (cuts the '5-zone world map' framing)
operator_decisions_pending.recommendation: B — preserves the 5-zone visual framing without inventing content. Cycle 2 will land 4 real zone YAMLs when fire/earth/metal/water elements ship.

operator_decisions_pending.question: CardStack adapter for harness cardType ('activation') ≠ honeycomb cardType (jani/caretaker_a/caretaker_b/transcendence)
options: A: Build a harnessCardToLayerInput(card) adapter in S4 (extends S4 scope by ~50 LOC; preserves visual continuity with prior layer work)
B: Render a harness-native placeholder card face in S4 (no CardStack dependency; defers art_anchor work cleanly to cycle 2; visual continuity lost)
C: Bind CardStack via a manual mapping where activation → 'caretaker_a' (visual lie that hides the type-mismatch; cycle 2 must clean up)
operator_decisions_pending.recommendation: A — small adapter is worth ~50 LOC to preserve CardStack visual continuity. Adapter contract becomes the bridge that cycle 2 evolves into full art_anchor integration.

operator_decisions_pending.question: Telemetry destination: resolve Q-SDD-5 now at PRD altitude, or cut FR-26/AC-13 from S5 until SDD resolves it
options: A: Resolve Q-SDD-5 now: JSONL trail at grimoires/loa/a2a/trajectory/telemetry-cycle-1-*.jsonl (matches predecessor-cycle observability pattern)
B: Cut FR-26 + AC-13 from S5; reintroduce after SDD resolves Q-SDD-5 (clean PRD; defers telemetry observability)
C: Resolve Q-SDD-5 now: route through Hivemind sink directly (richer integration; depends on hivemind setup)
operator_decisions_pending.recommendation: A — JSONL aligns with the predecessor cycle and is operator-readable. Cycle 2 can route to Hivemind once that integration is settled.

operator_decisions_pending.question: Add S0 calibration spike (predecessor cycle did this post-flatline per D9 in card-game PRD)
options: A: Add S0 = spike of one complex artifact (recommend: the AJV loader + 1 schema + 1 YAML end-to-end). Outcome calibrates Sprint 1's LOC + complexity estimates. ~1-day spike. Pre-S1 gate.
B: No S0 spike — the harness is well-specified; 5 sprints with current dependency-order is sufficient.
C: Lightweight S0: vendor schemas + run AJV against ONE YAML in a script (validate harness-codebase compatibility) WITHOUT building the full loader. Bounds the integration-risk surface.
operator_decisions_pending.recommendation: C — lightweight S0 surfaces the harness/AJV integration cost before committing to S1. Half-day max. Adds 0 LOC to net cycle budget. Closes the SCOPE-1 calibration concern at minimum overhead.
